title: ExampleScore
description: Quality scores for individual examples in an experiment run. Combines content-based scores (computed in SDK) with statistical scores (computed in backend).

id.description: Unique identifier for this score record

experiment_run_id.description: ID of the experiment run this example belongs to

example_id.description: Stable, deterministic ID for the example (format: ex_{hash}_{index})

content_uniqueness.description: Content-based uniqueness score (1.0 = completely unique, 0.0 = duplicate). Computed in SDK using TF-IDF similarity.

content_novelty.description: Content-based novelty score (1.0 = very novel, 0.0 = average). Computed in SDK by distance from dataset centroid.

informativeness.description: Statistical informativeness score based on variance across different configurations. High = example discriminates well between configs.

consistency.description: Statistical consistency score based on variance within same configuration. High = stable/reliable example.

difficulty.description: Statistical difficulty score based on correlation with config quality. High = difficult example that challenges good configs.

discriminative_power.description: Statistical measure of how well this example differentiates between configurations.

cost_efficiency.description: Information gain per unit of execution cost. High = valuable example relative to cost.

error_sensitivity.description: Sensitivity of metrics to execution variance. High = metrics vary significantly on this example.

predictive_value.description: Correlation between example performance and overall config quality. High = good predictor of general performance.

ambiguity.description: Variance in metrics independent of config quality. High = ambiguous/noisy example.

composite_score.description: Weighted composite of all individual scores. Overall quality indicator.

sample_count.description: Number of trials this example was evaluated in (more samples = more reliable scores)

algorithm_version.description: Version of the scoring algorithm used (e.g., '1.0.0')

created_at.description: Timestamp when this score was computed

updated_at.description: Timestamp when this score was last updated
